qids.subject: Algorithms
qids.prose: Suppose are sorted sequences having lengths respectively. They are to be merged into a single sequence by merging together two sequences at a time. The number of comparisons that will be needed in the worst case by the optimal algorithm for doing this is ____.

qids.prose: Kruskal's algorithm for finding a minimum spanning tree of a weighted graph with vertices and edges has the time complexity of: A. B. C. D. E.

qids.prose: Complexity of Kruskal's algorithm for finding the minimum spanning tree of an undirected graph containing vertices and edges if the edges are sorted is _______

qids.prose: How many minimum spanning trees does the following graph have? Draw them. (Weights are assigned to edges).
